steps.title: Set up the scale change
steps.content: Same actual region, but the larger map is $90\%$ longer per side — its side length is $1.9$ times the original. Stretching the map by factor $1.9$ means each actual mile is now drawn $1.9$ times longer on paper, so each inch on the new map represents fewer miles.

steps.title: Compute the new scale
steps.content:
Original: 1 inch = 13 miles. New: 1 inch = $\dfrac{13}{1.9}$ miles.
$$\dfrac{13}{1.9} \approx 6.84 \text{ miles}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
